methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A–stage autonomous systems company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised $14.4M to date. The comparable universe consists of eight private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker; no public peers with direct overlap exist.

Valuation analysis is constrained by limited same-stage comparable data. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set peers meeting stage and tier filters—only zero same-stage and one adjacent-stage comparable existed in the filtered dataset. Consequently, Stage-Progression methodology was the primary tool applied. Using total raised ($14.4M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, this method implies a valuation of $28.8M.

The triangulated range derived from available methods spans $24M (low) to $33M (high), with a median of $29M. The analysis rests on a single methodological pillar, making it vulnerable to model assumptions about appropriate Series A forward multiples in early-stage autonomy. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage peer data and the applicability of the 2.0x step-up assumption to frontier-tech subsectors where market maturity and investor appetite vary significantly. Additional resolution would require either expanded comparable data or direct revenue/traction metrics to anchor valuation to operational progress rather than financing history alone.
